wiki_en.summary: Zhou Tai, courtesy name Youping, was a military general serving under the warlord Sun Quan during the late Eastern Han dynasty and early Three Kingdoms period of China. He previously served under Sun Ce, Sun Quan's elder brother and predecessor.

sections.Lead:
Zhou Tai (pronunciationⓘ) (died c. 223), courtesy name Youping, was a military general serving under the warlord Sun Quan during the late Eastern Han dynasty and early Three Kingdoms period of China. He previously served under Sun Ce, Sun Quan's elder brother and predecessor.[2]

- Zhou Shao

- Zhou Cheng
sections.Death: Zhou Tai died sometime during the Huangwu era (222–229) in Sun Quan's reign.[1] His year of death was not specified, but the Australian sinologist Rafe de Crespigny estimated it to be around 223.[2]

wiki_zh.summary: 周泰（？—222年），字幼平，揚州九江下蔡（今安徽鳳臺）人。漢末三國時期孫策麾下的孫吳名將；是江東十二虎臣之一。與蔣欽是同鄉九江人，因護主有功，孫權賞賜予御幘青縑蓋，官至漢中太守、奮威將軍，封陵陽侯。

sections.Introduction: 周泰（？—222年），字幼平，揚州九江下蔡（今安徽鳳臺）人。漢末三國時期孫策麾下的孫吳名將；是江東十二虎臣之一。與蔣欽是同鄉九江人，因護主有功，孫權賞賜予御幘青縑蓋，官至漢中太守、奮威將軍，封陵陽侯。

Zhou Tai, styled Youping, was from Xiacai in Jiujiang commandery. Together with Jiang Qin, he served as Sun Ce’s bodyguard, and both men were known for their respectful performance of their duties and their deeds in battle. When Ce entered Kuaiji, Zhou Tai was made Major with a Separate Command, and given troops to command. Sun Quan took a liking to Zhou Tai’s character, and requested for Tai to be transferred to himself.
Once, while Sun Ce was out fighting Shanyue rebels from the six prefectures, Sun Quan was left in Xuan city with less than a thousand guards. Out of negligence, he did not set up defence posts. Several thousand of Shanyue rebels attacked. By the time Sun Quan mounted his horse, the bandits managed to surround him already. Their weapons, hitting here and there, slashed Sun Quan’s saddle, causing great panic all around. Only Zhou Tai, with spirits roused, defended Sun Quan with his body, emboldening those near him and making them able to fight again. When the bandits were finally dispersed, Zhou Tai was found with twelve deep wounds, which took a long time to heal. Had Zhou Tai not be there on that day, Sun Quan would have peri... (truncated)

dialogues.text:
「命此孺子為將，東吳休矣！公見彼所行乎？」泰曰：「吾聊以言試之，並無一計，安能破蜀也？」

次日，陸遜傳下號令，教諸將各處關防，牢守隘口，不許輕敵。
「目今安東將軍孫桓，乃主上之姪，見困於彝陵城中，內無糧草，外無救兵；請都督早施良策，救出孫桓，以安主上之心。
目今安東將軍孫桓，乃主上之姪，見困於彝陵城中，內無糧草，外無救兵；請都督早施良策，救出孫桓，以安主上之心。
吾守隴西二三十年，未嘗如此明察地理。公之所言，真神算也。公可速去。吾自攻此處寨柵。
吾視此等兵如兒戲耳。願同韓將軍分兩路擊之。如其不勝，甘當軍令。
命此孺子為將，東吳休矣！公見彼所行乎？
「吾守隴西二三十年，未嘗如此明察地理。
吾聊以言試之，並無一計，安能破蜀也？
主公在前，某在後，可以出圍。
獨斬賈充，少可以謝天下耳。
「惟有進於此者，不知其次。
惟有進於此者，不知其次。
「吾視此等兵如兒戲耳。
「某當權為先鋒破敵。
某當權為先鋒破敵。
「主公可隨泰殺出。
「都督自提兵至。
主公可隨泰殺出。
都督自提兵至。
「吾再救去。
